body.title: [dependency] Re-express the night-mode batch gate as a dimensional/policy concern in the resolver
body.description:
Follow-on from T-inference-migrate-consumers-cutover. route()'s night-mode gate (task_class=batch dispatches to local ollama only during 00:00-06:00, degrading to cloud outside the window to avoid daytime GPU contention) was DROPPED at cutover — resolve() has no time-of-day gating. Safe for now: no live consumer dispatches task_class=batch except Igor cognition (inference_gateway.py:414), which is DOWN. When batch dispatch returns, re-express the gate as a dimensional/policy predicate (urgency/time-bucket or a policy rule keyed on a time dimension) rather than a hardcoded hour check in the selector. Dependency: gated on batch dispatch becoming live again (Igor up, or a new batch consumer).

**Test plan:** red->green: a task_class=batch request outside 00:00-06:00 does not select a local-ollama connection (degrades to cloud), expressed via a dimension/policy not a hardcoded hour check.

[error] bus/ owns comms:// routing. Nothing outside bus/ speaks to IMAP directly. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] skeleton/ owns the MCP aggregator and flat-file registry. No Postgres dependency. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] devices/ contains one subdirectory per device; each is independently deployable. — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] `~/.unseen_university/logs/<device>/<stream>/` is the canonical log hierarchy — — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
[error] Every ticket closes proven, or declares itself unproven — no discriminator. A ticket that — applies: tags: architecture (source: CLAUDE.md#structural-rules)
